# Measurement and Learning Synthesis

## Core position

The campaign loop is only intelligent if results become durable memory.

The model should not read a dashboard and declare victory. It should separate:

- delivery metrics,
- engagement metrics,
- conversion metrics,
- revenue metrics,
- incrementality evidence,
- quality of lead/customer,
- long-term brand indicators.

## Metrics by campaign job

| Campaign job | Useful metrics | Misleading metrics |
|---|---|---|
| Demand capture | Conversion rate, CPA, qualified trials, pipeline | CTR alone |
| Demand creation | Engaged visits, recall proxies, branded search, content saves | Immediate ROAS only |
| Retargeting | Return visits, trial starts, objections resolved | View-through conversions without holdout |
| Activation | Setup complete, first value moment, retained usage | Signup count only |
| Pricing/offer test | Paid conversion, churn, ARPA, expansion | Trial starts only |
| Review/social proof | Assisted conversions, sales-cycle compression, stakeholder engagement | Last-click attribution only |

## Learning memo contract

Every experiment should end with:

```markdown
# Learning Memo

## Hypothesis
## What changed
## Result summary
## What we believe now
## Confidence level
## Why confidence is limited
## What to scale
## What to stop
## What to test next
## Memory updates required
```

## Autonomy rule

The agent can generate and interpret results, but it must write the uncertainty. Bad learning memory poisons future campaigns.
